rows.O: Failure of treatments performed to date. Treatment with the combination of PEMBROLIZUMAB and AXITINIB.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Renal cancer (type not further specified in the appellate decision); diagnosed in February 2023
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: In February 2023 the patient was diagnosed with renal cancer. On 14 March 2023 the patient underwent surgery at the Asistencia Médica de Maldonado. Prior treatments failed to halt the disease. The treating physician Dr. Sergio Aguiar then indicated the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib based on the Phase Three study KEYNOTE 426 (JCLIN ONCOL 2019).
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: NOT FOUND — document refers only to disease progression after prior treatments and the need for a new therapeutic regimen; no formal staging or ECOG Performance Status documented in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Prior treatments performed (specific regimen not named in the appellate decision); all failed to halt disease progression as stated by the treating physician Dr. Sergio Aguiar
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: The treating physician Dr. Sergio Aguiar stated in the clinical summary at page 4 of the record that treatments received by the patient did not manage to halt the disease; on the contrary, the disease progressed. On this basis, rotation to Pembrolizumab and Axitinib was indicated.
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dr. Sergio Aguiar and Dr. Natalia Dutra of the Tumor Committee of the medical institution indicated the combination of Pembrolizumab and Axitinib based on evidence from the Phase Three KEYNOTE 426 study (JCLIN ONCOL 2019), concluding it is the best therapeutic option given failure of prior treatments.
fields.URGENCY: The appellate court noted that the first-instance judgment ordered supply within 24 hours of notification; the court doctrine cited in the judgment states that in health-related amparo proceedings, treatments of this nature cannot be discontinued without serious harm to the patient and the prescription must be fulfilled immediately.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: The treating physician indicated that prior treatments had failed to halt disease progression and that the disease had advanced; without the indicated combination the disease would continue to progress.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court referenced the right to life, the right to health, the right to a better quality of life, and the right to a dignified survival, indicating that denial of the treatment placed these fundamental rights at risk.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab in combination with Axitinib; dose, route, and frequency as indicated by the treating physician and for the entire duration determined by the treating physician; no specific dose or interval stated in the appellate decision
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment in all its terms, ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab and Axitinib within 24 hours according to the treating physician's indications and for as long as the physician determines
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública acted with manifest illegitimacy by denying the combination of medications indicated by the treating physician without presenting any scientific basis for doing so, in violation of Article 44 second paragraph of the Uruguayan Constitution, which obliges the State to provide gratuitously the means of prevention and assistance to indigents and those lacking sufficient resources. The court applied the pro homine constitutional interpretive principle, holding that when two norms govern the same matter, the norm offering the greatest protection to the individual must prevail, and that the constitutional provision of Article 44 cannot be restricted by lower-ranking legislation. The court further held that the fact that both medications had been approved by the Ministerio de Salud Pública for commercialisation for the patient's type of pathology — even if not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos — demonstrated that the State had already studied and accepted their therapeutic value, and that fiscal or budgetary arguments presented without concrete evidentiary support cannot override the constitutionally protected rights to life and health of the individual patient.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Ley 16.011 (Amparo); Art. 7, Art. 44, Art. 72, Art. 332 of the Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Art. 25 of the Convención Americana de Derechos Humanos; Ley 13.751 (ratifying the Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos Sociales y Culturales); Art. 1 of Ley 16.519 (Protocolo de San Salvador); Leyes 18.211 and 18.335; Decreto 265/2006 (FTM); Néstor Pedro Sagüés, Acción de Amparo, p. 166; Daniel Ochs, La Acción de Amparo, p. 142; Brito, Revista de Derecho, Universidad de Montevideo, 2006, No. 10, p. 101; Van Rompaey, Reflexiones sobre el Derecho a la Salud y su Judicialización, Judicatura T. 52, p. 145; T.A.C. 2º Turno, Sentencia Definitiva No. 77/2019 of 15/5/2019; T.A.C. 4º Turno, Sentencia 144/2015; T.A.C. 1er Turno, Sentencia No. 228/2020; S.C.J. rows.N: Metastatic melanoma with right lymph node involvement (BRAF V600 mutated, adjuvant treatment)

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Metastatic cutaneous melanoma with right lymph node secondary involvement (metastatic melanoma with secondary ganglionic involvement on the right side); BRAF V600 mutation positive; adjuvant treatment setting
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient was diagnosed with metastatic melanoma with right lymph node secondary involvement. The case was discussed at the Tumor Committee. Treating physician Dr. Pablo Millán indicated treatment with Pembrolizumab in a specific dose to begin as soon as possible, with no other adequate treatment alternatives available. Pembrolizumab was approved by the FDA on 14 March 2017 and by the EMA on 7 September 2018; it has been registered in Uruguay since 28 February 2018 for cutaneous melanoma among other diseases, commercialised by Laboratorio Cibeles, and included in the Oncology Guidelines of the Cátedra de Oncología of the Facultad de Medicina of UDELAR.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Metastatic disease with lymph node secondary involvement; BRAF V600 mutation confirmed; adjuvant treatment context (post-resection); no formal staging designation or ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: No prior treatments described in the appellate decision; the case concerns adjuvant treatment following resection of metastatic melanoma
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — the case concerns adjuvant therapy; no prior systemic treatment failure is described
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dr. Pablo Millán, following discussion in the Tumor Committee, indicated Pembrolizumab in adjuvant setting for the patient's metastatic melanoma with BRAF V600 mutation, as no other adequate treatment alternatives exist. The Fondo Nacional de Recursos acknowledged that an alternative option could be BRAF-MEK inhibitors (Dabrafenib/Trametinib) in adjuvant criteria, but the patient, after being informed of benefits, adverse effects and possible toxicities, opted for immunotherapy with Pembrolizumab. No scientific reason was demonstrated justifying the absence of protocol for this specific clinical situation. The Pembrolizumab is included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for the systemic treatment of advanced cutaneous melanoma according to Ordinance No. 173/2020.
fields.URGENCY: The treating physician indicated that treatment must begin as soon as possible. The patient lacked sufficient resources to finance the high-cost doses.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: NOT FOUND explicitly; the court held that without the medication the patient would be denied the only therapy considered appropriate for his specific clinical situation
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that the denial of the medication violates the constitutional rights to life and health protected by Articles 7 and 44 of the Constitution, and that no reasonable explanation was provided for the exclusion of this clinical situation from the Fondo Nacional de Recursos coverage.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab; specific dose as indicated by the treating physician Dr. Pablo Millán; duration as determined by the treating physician; line of treatment: adjuvant (post-resection of metastatic melanoma with BRAF V600 mutation)
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones de Feria (acting as Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Primer Turno) confirmed the first-instance judgment in all its terms, ordering the Fondo Nacional de Recursos to supply Pembrolizumab within 24 hours according to the treating physician's indications and for as long as the physician determines; the first-instance court had also declared the lack of passive standing (legitimación pasiva) of the Ministerio de Salud Pública
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that Pembrolizumab is included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for the systemic treatment of advanced cutaneous melanoma without distinction, under Ordinance No. 173/2020, and therefore the Fondo Nacional de Recursos is a passively legitimate defendant in this action. The court found that the Fondo Nacional de Recursos provided no reasonable or evidence-based explanation for why it finances certain treatments but excludes others, specifically the exclusion of patients with BRAF mutation or in an adjuvant treatment setting, and that this absence of justification constitutes manifest illegitimacy violating the constitutional rights to life and health protected by Articles 7 and 44 of the Constitution and the equality principle of Article 8. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had previously declared unconstitutional Article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of Article 45 of Law 18.211 — the legal bases invoked by the Fondo Nacional de Recursos for its refusal — rendering those provisions inapplicable to the plaintiff.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Art. 7, Art. 8, Art. 44, Art. 72 of the Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Ley 16.011 (Amparo); Ordenanza No. 173/2020 (FTM inclusion of Pembrolizumab); Art. 9 of Decreto No. 130/2017; Sentencia No. 1114 of 24 November 2022 of the Suprema Corte de Justicia (declaring unconstitutional Art. 7 inc. 2 of Ley 18.335 and final paragraph of Art. 45 of Ley 18.211); arts. 197, 198 and 257 of the Código General del Proceso; arts. 688 Código Civil and 261 Código General del Proceso

rows.N: Triple-negative breast cancer (IDC G3 Score 9) with nodal involvement 90%
rows.O: Treated with radiotherapy and continues with hormone therapy. Neoadjuvant polychemotherapy based on Paclitaxel and Carboplatin with PEMBROLIZUMAB, followed by surgery and then adjuvant therapy.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Triple-negative breast cancer; invasive ductal carcinoma, grade three (histological grade three, Score 9), triple negative 90%; with nodal involvement confirmed by biopsy (secondary lymph node involvement); early-stage triple-negative breast cancer
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: In June 2023 the patient was diagnosed with triple-negative breast cancer following a biopsy that confirmed secondary lymph node involvement; anatomical pathology reported invasive ductal carcinoma histological grade three Score 9 triple negative 90%. The patient received radiotherapy and continued with hormone therapy. The case was discussed at the Asociación Española tumor board (ateneo). The proposed treatment plan was neoadjuvant polychemotherapy based on Paclitaxel and Carboplatin with Pembrolizumab, followed by surgery, and then adjuvant therapy with Pembrolizumab. Pembrolizumab is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for the patient's pathology. The patient is a widow, has a two-year-old daughter, and receives a pension from the Banco de Previsión Social of UYU 134,930 and UYU 20,038 from the Caja de Profesionales (documents at pages 35-38 of the record). The cost of the indicated dose (200 mg every 21 days) is approximately USD 7,200.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Early triple-negative breast cancer with nodal involvement; invasive ductal carcinoma grade three Score 9 triple negative 90%; no formal stage designation or ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.FUNCTIONAL_LIMITATIONS: The patient stated that the passage of time without receiving adequate treatment conspires against the possibilities of a quality survival and even against the very possibility of treatment, since if time passes and her condition deteriorates she will no longer be eligible to receive the medication.
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Radiotherapy (received); hormone therapy (ongoing at the time of the judicial action)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND in the sense of prior systemic anti-cancer treatment failure — the case involves neoadjuvant and adjuvant indication, not post-progression
fields.BIOMARKERS: Triple-negative (estrogen receptor negative, progesterone receptor negative, Human Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor 2 negative) — confirmed by anatomical pathology; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) status: NOT FOUND explicitly stated
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Secondary lymph node involvement (regional nodal disease confirmed by biopsy); no distant metastasis described
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dra. Stephanie Sterling indicated neoadjuvant polychemotherapy based on Paclitaxel and Carboplatin with Pembrolizumab, followed by surgery, and then adjuvant Pembrolizumab. Independent expert witness Dra. Dahiana Amarillo (Medical Oncologist, Assistant of the Clinical Oncology Service and Basic Medicine Department) concluded that the indication of Pembrolizumab treatment for this patient is correct and adjusted to the available evidence for this case of breast cancer. She concluded that adding Pembrolizumab to chemotherapy increases the rate of complete pathological response by 13%, and increases three-year event-free survival by 7%. There are no scientific reasons explaining the absence of its inclusion in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos, and without the treatment the patient would present a lower response rate and a 7% higher risk of local or distant relapse (event-free survival). The Phase Three clinical trial demonstrated that among patients with early triple-negative breast cancer, the percentage of patients with a complete pathological response was significantly higher among those who received Pembrolizumab plus neoadjuvant chemotherapy compared to those who received placebo plus neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
fields.URGENCY: The patient stated she is currently in a position to receive the medication; however, the mere passage of time without receiving adequate treatment conspires against the possibilities of a quality survival and even against the very possibility of treatment, since if her condition deteriorates she will no longer be eligible to receive the medication. The amparo court doctrine cited requires that drug prescriptions be fulfilled immediately.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Without the treatment the patient would present a lower response rate and a 7% higher risk of local or distant relapse; the disease would progress with reduced treatment options.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that denial of the medication violates the rights to life and health (Articles 7 and 44 of the Constitution); the expert witness confirmed the medication is the only option that can provide a greater disease-free life and halt disease progression.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg every 21 days intravenously (cost stated as approximately USD 7,200 per dose); administered as part of neoadjuvant polychemotherapy in combination with Paclitaxel and Carboplatin, followed by surgery, and then adjuvant Pembrolizumab; according to treating physician's indications and for the duration determined by the treating physician
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment (Sentencia Definitiva No. 58/2023 of 10 August 2023 by Dra. Ana Bello) ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab according to the treating physician's indications; the first-instance court had upheld the lack of passive standing of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos, which was not appealed
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública acted with manifest illegitimacy by denying without scientific justification the supply of Pembrolizumab to the plaintiff, who has no economic resources to acquire it privately, thereby violating the constitutional rights to life and health under Articles 44 and 72 of the Constitution. The court relied on expert testimony from Dra. Dahiana Amarillo establishing that Pembrolizumab is the only treatment offering a greater disease-free life and halting disease progression, and that there are no scientific reasons justifying its absence from the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 920/2023 of 14 September 2023 — the final paragraph of Article 45 of Law 18.211 and paragraph 2 of Article 7 of Law 18.335, removing the legislative basis for the state's refusal and reinforcing the direct applicability of Article 44 of the Constitution.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Art. 7, Art. 44, Art. 72, Art. 332 of the Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Art. 8 of the Constitution (equality); Ley 16.011 (Amparo); Art. 12 of the Pacto Internacional de Derechos Económicos, Sociales y Culturales (ratified by Ley 13.751); Art. 1 of Ley 16.519 (Protocolo de San Salvador); Leyes 18.211 and 18.335; S.C.J. rows.N: Papillary renal cell carcinoma type 2, grade 3 (WHO/ISUP), pathological study pT3 a N1, neoplastic proliferation of papillary architecture

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Clear cell renal cancer (as described by the treating physician Dra. Isabel Alonso); anatomical pathology of 2020 nephrectomy specimen confirmed papillary renal cell carcinoma type 2, grade 3 (WHO/ISUP classification), pathological staging pT3a N1; recurrence confirmed in March 2023 with anatomical pathology of retroperitoneal fragments demonstrating neoplastic proliferation of papillary architecture morphologically compatible with the antecedent papillary renal cell carcinoma; disseminated renal cancer
fields.PATIENT_AGE: 66 years old at the time the amparo action was filed
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: In 2020 the patient underwent left nephrectomy; anatomical pathology confirmed papillary renal cell carcinoma type 2, grade 3 (WHO/ISUP), pT3a N1. Following staging, the patient was considered very high risk and had a very good post-surgical evolution. The patient began adjuvant interferon therapy. In March 2023 the patient consulted due to vomiting, headaches and diarrhoea; computed tomography showed a solid-cystic left retroperitoneal mass in close contact with the psoas muscle and the posterior and lateral left sector. Anatomical pathology of the examined fatty omental fragments confirmed neoplastic proliferation of papillary architecture morphologically compatible with the antecedent papillary renal cell carcinoma. At that point, Pembrolizumab and Axitinib were indicated. The patient is retired and receives a monthly pension of UYU 30,560 after loan deductions from the Caja Nacional; the patient's husband is also retired and receives UYU 85,691 from the Banco de Previsión Social. The cost of Pembrolizumab (Keytruda, commercialised by Laboratorio Cibeles) is UYU 330,000 per dose (approximately USD 7,500), at 200 mg every 21 days (two 100 mg vials). Axitinib (commercialised by Pfizer) costs UYU 1,889.96 per 5 mg, with a daily requirement of 10 mg.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Disseminated renal cancer; recurrent papillary renal cell carcinoma type 2; original pathological staging pT3a N1; recurrence with left retroperitoneal mass; very high risk classification post-surgery; no formal ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Left nephrectomy (2020); adjuvant interferon therapy (initiated after post-surgical staging); Pazopanib and Sunitinib noted by expert witness as alternative Fondo Nacional de Recursos-funded options but considered appropriate only for good-risk patients according to clinical trials and international guidelines
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: Adjuvant interferon therapy; recurrence of papillary renal cell carcinoma documented by computed tomography and anatomical pathology in March 2023, demonstrating progression of disease despite prior treatment
fields.BIOMARKERS: NOT FOUND explicitly; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR), Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK), ROS1, KRAS, BRAF, Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1), Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Left retroperitoneal mass (solid-cystic) in close contact with the psoas muscle; posterior and lateral left sector involvement confirmed by computed tomography and anatomical pathology
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dra. Isabel Alonso (oncologist, CAMDEL) indicated Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib and ratified her report in the hearing, explaining the pertinence of the treatment for the patient's situation and the need to administer it as soon as possible. Independent expert witness Dr. Lyber Alejandro Saldombide Mauad (medical oncologist) concluded at the 23 June 2023 hearing that the indication of Pembrolizumab plus Axitinib for this patient is correct and adjusted to international and national consensus for the treatment of disseminated renal cancer; it is the precise indication because there are few effective treatment strategies available and this combination has demonstrated benefits in response rate, disease-free survival and overall survival. The Keynote study supports the benefits of the combined plan; with the Pembrolizumab scheme, response rates are superior to Sunitinib (59.3% versus 39.7%). The plan is considered first-line for this patient by ESMO, NCCN and the 2022 Oncology Guidelines of the Clinical Oncology Service of UDELAR. Without the treatment the patient is deprived of the benefits in disease-free survival, response rates and overall survival with the best available therapeutic option.
fields.URGENCY: The expert witness stated the patient must receive the treatment as soon as possible. The document states the case has the most absolute urgency, since the passage of time without the adequate medication substantially affects the patient's health, and if treatment is not initiated promptly the patient will be deprived of the possibility of a dignified life.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Without the treatment, the patient is deprived of benefits in disease-free survival, response rates and overall survival; delay in treatment substantially affects health status.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The treating physician indicated that without the treatment the patient will be deprived of the possibility of a dignified life; the court held that the rights to life and health are at stake and cannot be subordinated to merely economic and financial reasons.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg (two 100 mg vials) every 21 days intravenously, in combination with Axitinib 10 mg per day orally; first-line treatment for disseminated papillary renal cell carcinoma; duration as prescribed by the treating physicians
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Sexto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment (Sentencia Definitiva No. 51/2023 of 26 June 2023 by Dr. Gustavo Nicastro), ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to provide the patient with Pembrolizumab and Axitinib at the prescribed doses and for the duration prescribed by the treating physicians within 24 continuous hours of notification; the lack of passive standing of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos was confirmed as unchallenged and final
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública acted with manifest illegitimacy by failing to scientifically examine the patient's individual case when petitioned, and by omitting a scientifically grounded pronouncement on the request for the combination of medications; this omission constitutes manifest illegitimacy independent of whether the Ministerio is obligated to provide any specific medication, because it must always justify its acts with adequate reasons. The court found that the treating physician's indication was not scientifically challenged, that no scientific reason was presented for the non-inclusion of the medications in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for this pathology, and that the rights to life and health cannot be subordinated to economic and financial reasons. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 791 of 17 August 2023 — the provisions of Law 18.335 and Law 18.211 invoked by the Ministerio de Salud Pública, rendering them inapplicable to the plaintiff.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Art. 7, Art. 8, Art. 44, Art. 72 of the Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Ley 16.011 (Amparo); Arts. 183, 184, 157 and 158 of the Código General del Proceso; Art. 25 of the Convención Americana de Derechos Humanos; Leyes 18.211 and 18.335; Decreto 265/006; Decreto No. 130/2017; Art. 462 of Ley No. 19.335 (as amended by Art. 299 of Ley No. 20.075); S.C.J. Sentencia No. 791 of 17/8/2023 (unconstitutionality of Art. 7 inc. 2 of Ley 18.335 and final paragraph of Art. 45 of Ley 18.211); T.A.C. 1º Turno, Sentencia 203/2014; Luis A. Viera, La ley de Amparo; Ordenanza 173/2020 (FTM); 2022 National Oncology Guidelines of UDELAR Oncology Service; Keynote study; ESMO; NCCN; Arts. 56 Código General del Proceso and 688 Código Civil

rows.N: Adjuvant treatment of ulcerated malignant melanoma Clark level V with metastasis in 2 of 4 lymph nodes, with periganglionic invasion
rows.O: PEMBROLIZUMAB adjuvant therapy for one year

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Malignant melanoma, ulcerated, Clark level Five, with metastasis in two of four lymph nodes with periganglionic invasion; cutaneous melanoma with nodal involvement; adjuvant treatment setting post-sentinel lymph node biopsy and lymph node dissection
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient is 61 years old and attended COSEM. The condition began with a lesion on the back; anatomical pathology confirmed malignant ulcerated melanoma at Clark level Five. Wide excision of the sentinel lymph node was performed; anatomical pathology of the sentinel node reported metastasis in two of four lymph nodes with periganglionic invasion. Positron emission tomography (PET scan) reported focal, mild hypermetabolism at the cutaneous and subcutaneous level of the back in the interscapular region, in which persistence of active tumour tissue could not be excluded. In this situation, adjuvant treatment with Pembrolizumab for one year was indicated. The drug is included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos but not for this adjuvant treatment situation. The recommended dose is 200 mg every 21 days, at a cost of USD 7,102. The patient's sole income is a retirement pension of UYU 14,717.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Malignant melanoma Clark level Five; ulcerated; nodal involvement (two of four lymph nodes positive with periganglionic invasion); residual metabolic activity on PET scan in the interscapular region; post-surgical adjuvant setting; no formal ECOG Performance Status or TNM stage designation stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Wide excision of the primary melanoma lesion; sentinel lymph node biopsy and extended lymph node dissection (ampliación del ganglio centinela)
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — the case involves adjuvant treatment to prevent recurrence; no prior systemic treatment failure described
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): NOT FOUND; BRAF mutation status: NOT FOUND; Microsatellite Instability: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Lymph node metastasis: two of four lymph nodes positive with periganglionic invasion; PET scan: focal mild hypermetabolism in the interscapular dorsal region (cutaneous and subcutaneous), cannot exclude persistence of active tumour tissue
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dr. Diego Touya (COSEM Tumor Committee) indicated adjuvant Pembrolizumab for one year. At the hearing, Dr. Touya testified about the benefits of the medication for this patient specifically in adjuvant treatment: the goal of this preventive treatment is to prevent the disease from extending to the rest of the organism and to prevent dissemination. The international study KEYNOTE 054 (EORTC1325/KEYNOTE-054) determined that this type of treatment is very beneficial in this type of patient, demonstrating benefit in recurrence-free survival; both the EMA and the Oncology Guidelines of the Facultad de Medicina of UDELAR approved its use for this disease.
fields.URGENCY: The patient requires Pembrolizumab adjuvant therapy urgently for one year to avoid relapse; there is a risk that the disease will reappear and become incurable; if the disease reappears and disseminates, it will very likely be too late to initiate any treatment.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Risk that the disease reappears and becomes incurable if adjuvant treatment is not administered.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that if the disease reappears and disseminates, it will very likely be too late to initiate any treatment; the rights to life and health are at stake.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg every 21 days intravenously; adjuvant therapy for one year; according to treating physician's indications
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — on appeal, the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno modified the first-instance judgment (Sentencia Definitiva No. 69/2023 of 11 August 2023 by Dr. Darwin Rampoldi): the first-instance court had ordered the Fondo Nacional de Recursos to supply Pembrolizumab for one year; the appellate court revoked the condemnation of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos (declaring its lack of passive standing) and instead condemned the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab to the plaintiff for one year within 24 hours as indicated by the treating physician
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública approved the commercial sale of Pembrolizumab in Uruguay for the patient's type of disease, and that having approved its sale, the Ministerio must be presumed to have studied and accepted its therapeutic value for this type of pathology; consequently, denying its supply without any scientific basis constitutes manifest illegitimacy in violation of Article 44 of the Constitution, which obliges the State to provide gratuitously the means of prevention and assistance to those lacking sufficient economic resources. The court reversed the first-instance condemnation of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos because Pembrolizumab was not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for adjuvant treatment of resectable melanoma — as the Ministerio's Ordinance 173/2020 covered only the systemic treatment of advanced cutaneous melanoma, not adjuvant treatment — and the Fondo Nacional de Recursos is a financing rather than an assistance entity that can only provide medications expressly assigned to it by the Ministerio. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 905 of 14 September 2023 — the relevant provisions of Laws 18.335 and 18.211, rendering them inapplicable to the plaintiff.

The Ministerio de Salud Pública did not question the appropriateness of the prescribed medications for the patient's condition; it maintained total silence regarding the patient's personal condition in both the contestación de la demanda and on appeal. fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that the rights to life and health under Articles 7 and 44 of the Constitution and international human rights instruments must be protected, and that the Ministerio's omission constitutes an anti-legal conduct endangering the patient's survival.

fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Cuarto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment (Sentencia No. 38 of 19 May 2023 by Dra. Fabiana Weisz) ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab and Axitinib in the terms and conditions stipulated by the treating physician team; the court specified that the compliance deadline is immediate; the lack of passive standing of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos was confirmed
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública maintained total silence concerning the patient's personal condition at both stages of litigation, failing to provide any scientific justification for refusing the indicated medications, which constitutes manifest illegitimacy under Article 1 of Law 16.011. The court reasoned that the Constitution is the supreme norm and that Article 44 cannot be disregarded based on utilitarian criteria that leave a person without necessary pharmacological coverage, and that the Ministerio's refusal was based solely on the fact that the drug is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos or is registered for a different pathology — an argument the court has repeatedly rejected. The Suprema Corte de Justicia declared unconstitutional — by majority with partial dissents — Article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of Article 45 of Law 18.211, removing the legislative basis for the Ministerio's refusal.

fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that all elements for granting the amparo are present: a diagnosed illness, a medical recommendation that must be followed, insufficient resources of the patient, no contestation of the need for the procedure by the defendant, and values of the Human Rights Block being flagrantly violated. The court confirmed the supply of Pembrolizumab by the Fondo Nacional de Recursos because the Ministerio de Salud Pública had included Pembrolizumab in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma — which is precisely the patient's disease — without any inclusion or exclusion criterion as to Programmed Death-Ligand 1 percentage, while the Fondo Nacional de Recursos had unilaterally established the 50% or more Programmed Death-Ligand 1 criterion in its coverage protocol; the court found no justification for this restriction. The court also held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública is solely responsible for the supply of Pemetrexed since it is registered but not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos, and its omission in providing it to a resident in special need constitutes manifest illegitimacy.

He lacks sufficient resources to acquire the drug by his own means. Pembrolizumab is approved by the FDA and the EMA, has been registered in Uruguay before the Ministerio de Salud Pública since February 2018, is commercialised by Cibeles S.A., and incorporated into the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos since February 2020 for certain pathologies different from colon cancer. The treating physician and independent expert witness Dra. Dahiana Amarillo both testified in support of the treatment, referencing published scientific literature and approval by the FDA and the EMA.

The Ministerio de Salud Pública did not present scientific reasons justifying the non-inclusion of Pembrolizumab in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for the patient's pathology; its refusal was based entirely on the drug not being included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for colon cancer.

fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Quinto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab, and revoked the condemnation of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos, declaring its lack of passive standing; three ministers voted to revoke the Fondo Nacional de Recursos condemnation; one minister (Dra. Gabriela Rodríguez Marichal) dissented in part and would have maintained the Fondo Nacional de Recursos condemnation
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court majority held that the Fondo Nacional de Recursos lacks passive standing because Pembrolizumab is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for colon cancer with Microsatellite Stable, and the Fondo Nacional de Recursos can only cover medications expressly assigned to it; Laws 19.889 and 19.924 did not modify its competences. With respect to the Ministerio de Salud Pública, the court unanimously held that the refusal constitutes manifest illegitimacy because: the Suprema Corte de Justicia declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 186/2023 of 9 March 2023 — Article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of Article 45 of Law 18.211; the Ministerio presented criteria it described as scientific but never specified them; it failed to conduct an individualised study of the patient's situation; and Article 44 of the Constitution obliges the State to provide gratuitously to those without resources the means of prevention and assistance, an obligation that cannot be restricted by the Ministerio's generic defences.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Lung cancer, Stage Four (adenocarcinoma poorly differentiated), immunophenotypic profile compatible with primary lung adenocarcinoma; ganglionic Stage Four with Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) positive at 40%; non-small cell lung carcinoma type; no Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) mutation or AML4-ALK translocation detected
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (Mr. Nelson Jiménez) is 67 years old with a history of smoking (ex-smoker), Diabetes Mellitus Insulinorequirente (DMIR) and Arterioesclerosis (AEA). In 2022 he consulted in Brazil due to a tumour at the neck level and exertional dyspnoea. A computed tomography scan (TAC CTAP) showed multiple carotid, mediastinal and pelvic adenopathies. Biopsy of the neck adenopathy reported adenocarcinoma poorly differentiated with immunophenotypic profile compatible with primary lung adenocarcinoma. He received six cycles of polychemotherapy with the Carboplatin/Paclitaxel regimen without response. In September 2022 in the city of Artigas, he consulted due to increase of neck tumour, dysphonia and denied dyspnoea. A new TAC CTAP showed adenopathies of the neck, mediastinal, axillary, inguinal regions and a lesion of 27 mm at the L5D level without bone lesions. A new biopsy for molecular profile and PD-L1 was performed, reporting ganglionic metastasis of poorly differentiated adenocarcinoma of the lung. He was diagnosed with pulmonary neoplasm at ganglionic Stage Four and PD-L1 positive at 40%. Treating physician Dr. Luis Goncalves (Hospital de Artigas) indicated second-line Pembrolizumab based on the Keynote 010 study, post first-line platinum-based chemotherapy. Monthly income: UYU 11,000 (retirement pension). Cost of Pembrolizumab: UYU 189,596 per 100 mg.
fields.SYMPTOMS: Neck tumour; exertional dyspnoea (in 2022); dysphonia; increase of neck tumour mass (September 2022)
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Lung cancer ganglionic Stage Four; non-small cell lung carcinoma (adenocarcinoma poorly differentiated); no Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) mutation; no AML4-ALK translocation; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) positive at 40%; no ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.COMORBIDITIES: Former smoker (ex-fumador); Diabetes Mellitus Insulinorequirente (DMIR); Arterioesclerosis (AEA)
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Six cycles of polychemotherapy with Carboplatin/Paclitaxel regimen (first-line); no response — disease progressed
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: Six cycles of Carboplatin/Paclitaxel polychemotherapy administered without response; the disease progressed as documented by the September 2022 computed tomography showing increased neck tumour and new adenopathies and a lesion at L5D; the expert witness Dra. Dahiana Amarillo confirmed the first-line chemotherapy was not effective and the disease advanced.
fields.BIOMARKERS: Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): positive at 40% (below the Fondo Nacional de Recursos coverage criterion of 50% or more); Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): no mutation detected; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): no AML4-ALK translocation detected; no biological therapy target identified; Microsatellite Instability status: NOT FOUND; KRAS: NOT FOUND; BRAF: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Carotid adenopathies (neck); mediastinal adenopathies; axillary adenopathies; inguinal adenopathies; lesion of 27 mm at L5D level (lumbar); no bone lesions on imaging
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dr. Luis Goncalves (oncologist, Hospital de Artigas, attending since September 2022) indicated that the patient has no targetable biological therapy and has a PD-L1 of 40%. The Keynote 010 study has demonstrated that in patients with lung cancer who have progressed under first-line platinum-based chemotherapy and present a PD-L1 of 40%, second-line Pembrolizumab provides improvement in overall survival, good objective response rates, and the drug is well tolerated with a good safety profile; without the medication, life expectancy is very short. Expert witness Dra. Dahiana Amarillo confirmed that while the patient received initial chemotherapy, it was ineffective and the disease advanced; the Keynote 010 study demonstrates an improvement in overall survival of more than four months compared to chemotherapy; Pembrolizumab is better tolerated and less toxic than Docetaxel; the treatment is adequate and consistent with national and international guidelines; the PD-L1 percentage is not a limiting factor; no scientific reasons justify the non-inclusion in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos.
fields.URGENCY: The court found the first-instance judgment granting supply within five days was appropriate; the Ministerio de Salud Pública failed to conduct a scientific analysis of the patient's individual case and omitted a motivated pronouncement, which is manifestly illegitimate.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Without the medication, life expectancy is very short according to the treating physician Dr. Luis Goncalves.
fields.RISK_DEATH: Life expectancy is very short without the medication; the treating physician and expert witness both confirmed the urgency.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 180 mg every 21 days intravenously (second-line, post first-line platinum-based chemotherapy); based on Keynote 010 study; duration as indicated by the treating physician
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Sexto Turno confirmed the first-instance judgment ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab within five days according to the treating physician's indications and for as long as indicated; the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' lack of passive standing was confirmed and not appealed
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Ministerio de Salud Pública acted with manifest illegitimacy by failing to conduct any scientific examination of the patient's individual case when petitioned, and by delegating its response to the Division of Legal Services which merely deferred to registry information and the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' coverage criteria — thereby omitting a motivated pronouncement on the specific clinical situation. The court emphasised that even when the Ministerio is not obligated to provide any specific medication, it must justify its acts with adequate reasons so that the legitimacy or illegitimacy of its functions can be distinguished, and that this administrative silence violates the constitutional right of every resident to know the grounds for public authority decisions affecting them directly (Article 72 of the Constitution). The Ministerio's failure to consider the particular case — where Pembrolizumab is included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for advanced non-small cell lung carcinoma but the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' coverage criterion requires 50% or more PD-L1 — provided additional reason to examine the patient's situation, given that the patient's pathology falls within the definition but with a PD-L1 of 40%.
fields.COURT_CITATIONS: Art. 44, Art. 72 of the Constitución de la República Oriental del Uruguay; Ley 16.011 (Amparo); Art. 4 of Ley 18.211; Arts. 44 and 72 of the Constitution; Decreto No. 130/2017 Arts. 11-13; Ordenanza No. 173 of 28 February 2020 (FTM inclusion of Pembrolizumab for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma); T.A.C. 6º Turno, Sentencia 50/2014; Arts. 261 and 56 of the Código General del Proceso; Keynote 010 study

rows.N: Folliculotropic mycosis fungoides with large cell transformation

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Folliculotropic mycosis fungoides with large cell transformation; cutaneous T-cell lymphoma; rare and highly aggressive variant
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient is 20 years old and suffers from folliculotropic mycosis fungoides with large cell transformation. Treating physician Dra. Sofía Grille indicated Pembrolizumab, a high-cost drug included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos but not for the patient's specific pathology. Pembrolizumab is the only alternative available to the patient; no other treatment could halt the disease. The patient lacks sufficient economic resources to finance the treatment. The first-instance court (Dr. Alejandro Recarey Mastrángelo) condemned both the Fondo Nacional de Recursos and the Ministerio de Salud Pública in solidum to provide Pembrolizumab within five days as required by the treating physician.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Folliculotropic mycosis fungoides with large cell transformation; described as the only therapeutic alternative; no formal staging or ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: NOT FOUND explicitly; the Fondo Nacional de Recursos stated the drug is not registered in Uruguay for the patient's pathology; no prior specific treatments named in the appellate text
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND explicitly described; Pembrolizumab is described as the only alternative, implying prior treatments were either unavailable, exhausted or inadequate
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dra. Sofía Grille indicated Pembrolizumab as the only therapeutic alternative for the patient. The Fondo Nacional de Recursos argued the drug is not registered for this pathology. The Ministerio de Salud Pública argued the drug is not registered for the patient's pathology and that supply would require non-compliance with current pharmaceutical legislation. The court held that Pembrolizumab is a registered drug in Uruguay and that the issue concerns supply to this specific patient rather than inclusion in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos; the constitutional obligation under Article 44 requires the State to provide the medication to a person lacking sufficient resources.
fields.URGENCY: The first-instance court ordered supply within five days under astreintes; the appellate court confirmed that the prescription is correct and the constitutional obligation is immediate.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: Pembrolizumab is described as the only therapeutic alternative available to the patient; denial of the treatment means total absence of disease control.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that the rights to life and health of the patient are at stake and that the constitutional obligation under Article 44 requires the State to provide the means of assistance to those lacking resources; Pembrolizumab is the only known cognisable therapeutic alternative.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab; dose, route and frequency as indicated by the treating physician Dra. Sofía Grille; duration as determined by the treating physician
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Primer Turno by unanimous vote: (1) revoked the first-instance judgment insofar as it condemned the Fondo Nacional de Recursos, declaring its lack of passive standing and dismissing the action against it; (2) confirmed the first-instance judgment in all other respects, maintaining the condemnation of the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab as required by the treating physician
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Fondo Nacional de Recursos lacks passive standing in cases where the requested medication is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for the patient's specific pathology, since as a non-state public entity it can only perform activities within its legally defined scope of competence, and its patrimony is exclusively destined to the purposes established by its founding law; Laws 19.889 and 19.924 did not modify this competence. With respect to the Ministerio de Salud Pública, the court confirmed the condemnation on the basis that the request concerns the supply of Pembrolizumab to this specific patient rather than its inclusion in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos; Article 44 paragraph 2 of the Constitution obliges the State to provide gratuitously the means of assistance to those lacking sufficient resources, and the State cannot avoid this obligation by arguing that the drug is not in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos or lacks registration for the specific pathology when it is a registered drug; the Suprema Corte de Justicia declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 7/2023 of 2 February 2023 — the relevant provisions of Laws 18.335 and 18.211, and the court found that Pembrolizumab is the only cognisable therapeutic alternative for the patient.

rows.O: Having already undergone three lines of drug treatment, none of which halted disease progression, the treating physicians indicated a fourth line of treatment based on PEMBROLIZUMAB at a dose of 200 mg every 21 days.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Hodgkin lymphoma; relapsed/refractory disease after three lines of treatment
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient is 22 years old and has suffered from Hodgkin lymphoma since March 2021. Three lines of drug treatment were administered, none of which halted the progression of the disease. The treating physicians indicated a fourth line of treatment with Pembrolizumab at 200 mg every 21 days as the only adequate therapeutic alternative to palliate the disease and achieve greater dignified survival. Pembrolizumab is registered for commercialisation in Uruguay, indicated by international literature and by international organisms for the treatment of Hodgkin lymphoma, but is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for this indication. The patient cannot afford the treatment by her own economic means. The first-instance court (Dr. Gustavo Iribarren) condemned both the Fondo Nacional de Recursos and the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab within 24 hours.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Hodgkin lymphoma, relapsed/refractory after three lines of treatment; disease progression documented despite all three prior treatment lines; described as requiring a fourth line; no formal staging or ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: Three prior lines of drug treatment (specific regimens not named in the appellate text); all three lines failed to halt disease progression
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: Three prior lines of drug treatment administered from March 2021 onwards; none of the three lines managed to halt disease progression; the patient remained with active progressing Hodgkin lymphoma despite three treatment lines
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physicians indicated fourth-line Pembrolizumab 200 mg every 21 days as the only adequate therapeutic alternative to palliate the disease and achieve greater dignified survival. Pembrolizumab is registered in Uruguay and indicated by international literature and international organisations for Hodgkin lymphoma. The patient performed her duty by submitting to all indicated treatment lines; having exhausted accessible options, she is entitled to demand from the State the means of assistance she requires but cannot afford. No scientific reasons were presented by the Ministerio de Salud Pública or the Fondo Nacional de Recursos justifying the refusal.
fields.URGENCY: The first-instance court ordered supply within 24 hours under economic astreintes; the patient has fulfilled her constitutional duty to seek treatment and has no other options.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: The disease has progressed through three prior treatment lines; without fourth-line Pembrolizumab the disease will continue to progress with no further treatment options.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that the rights to life, health, dignity and quality of life are protected by Articles 7, 44, 72, 82 and 332 of the Constitution and by international human rights instruments; the patient's very survival and dignity are at stake.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg every 21 days intravenously; fourth line of treatment for relapsed/refractory Hodgkin lymphoma; duration as indicated by the treating physicians
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Tercero Turno (specially integrated): (1) revoked the condemnation of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos, declaring its lack of passive standing and dismissing the action against it; (2) confirmed the condemnation of the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab; without special costs in the instance. One minister (Dr. Fernando Tovagliare) dissented regarding the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' lack of passive standing and would have maintained its condemnation.
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The majority of the appellate court held that the Fondo Nacional de Recursos lacks passive standing because Pembrolizumab is not included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for Hodgkin lymphoma — it was included only for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma and advanced cutaneous melanoma — and as a non-state public entity the Fondo Nacional de Recursos can only perform activities within its legally defined competence; Laws 19.889 and 19.924 did not modify this competence, as the legislative history confirms these provisions concern financing mechanisms rather than extensions of coverage obligations. The court confirmed the Ministerio de Salud Pública's condemnation because: Article 44 paragraph 2 of the Constitution imposes a prestational obligation to provide health care assistance to those lacking sufficient resources; the patient has fulfilled her constitutional duty by submitting to all three prior treatment lines; the Ministerio failed to present any concrete scientific justification for refusing the only available fourth-line treatment; and the refusal based solely on the drug not being in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos for this pathology constitutes manifest illegitimacy. The Suprema Corte de Justicia had declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 813/2022 — the relevant provisions of Laws 18.335 and 18.211.

fields.PRIMARY_DIAGNOSIS: Lung cancer, locally advanced; non-small cell lung carcinoma (the document describes the treating physician's plan as chemotherapy plus Pembrolizumab based on the Keynote 407 study, which concerns squamous cell carcinoma; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) positive at 10%; Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) and Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK) both negative; surgical intervention excluded due to ganglionic disease
fields.CLINICAL_HISTORY: The patient (Mr. AA) is 72 years old and suffers from lung cancer. Treating physician Dra. Dahiana Amarillo (oncologist, Hospital Evangélico) is treating him. Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR) and Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK) testing was negative. Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) is 10%. The patient's situation was discussed at the Tumor Committee and chemotherapy plus Pembrolizumab was indicated based on the Phase Three randomised Keynote 407 study, which demonstrated a considerable increase in overall survival (15.9 versus 11.3 months) independently of PD-L1 level, as well as better progression-free survival (6.4 versus 4.8 months). Administrative petitions were presented to the Fondo Nacional de Recursos and the Ministerio de Salud Pública. Pembrolizumab is manufactured by Merck Sharp and Dohme, represented in Uruguay by CIBELES; price per 100 mg unit: UYU 152,656; the patient requires 200 mg every 21 days (UYU 305,312 per dose). Monthly pension income: UYU 34,170. No assets or savings. Pembrolizumab was included in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos under the Fondo Nacional de Recursos coverage by Ordinance 173/2020 for advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma and for advanced cutaneous melanoma systemic treatment; however, the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' coverage protocol requires Programmed Death-Ligand 1 at 50% or more for lung cancer coverage, which the patient does not meet.
fields.DISEASE_SEVERITY: Locally advanced lung cancer; ganglionic disease precluding surgery; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1) at 10% (below the Fondo Nacional de Recursos coverage criterion of 50% or more); no formal TNM staging designation or ECOG Performance Status stated in the appellate text
fields.PRIOR_TREATMENTS: NOT FOUND — the document describes the first treatment indication; no prior systemic anti-cancer treatment described
fields.THERAPEUTIC_FAILURE: NOT FOUND — chemotherapy plus Pembrolizumab is the first-line treatment being requested; no prior treatment failure described
fields.BIOMARKERS: Epidermal Growth Factor Receptor (EGFR): negative; Anaplastic Lymphoma Kinase (ALK): negative; Programmed Death-Ligand 1 (PD-L1): positive at 10%; Microsatellite Instability: NOT FOUND; ROS1: NOT FOUND; KRAS: NOT FOUND; BRAF: NOT FOUND
fields.METASTASIS_SITES: Ganglionic involvement (mediastinal disease precluding surgery); specific regional or distant metastatic sites not detailed in the appellate text
fields.MEDICAL_JUSTIFICATION: Treating physician Dra. Dahiana Amarillo indicated chemotherapy plus Pembrolizumab based on the Phase Three randomised Keynote 407 study, demonstrating overall survival improvement of 15.9 versus 11.3 months independently of Programmed Death-Ligand 1 level, and better progression-free survival (6.4 versus 4.8 months); results with chemotherapy alone would be inferior. The doctor also confirmed she does not know the scientific reasons for providing the drug only to patients with Programmed Death-Ligand 1 at 50% or more. The Ministerio de Salud Pública failed to analyse the patient's case from a scientific standpoint, limiting its response to procedural information about drug registration and deferring to the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' coverage criteria — constituting manifest illegitimacy.
fields.URGENCY: Administrative petitions were made to both defendants without satisfactory response; the appellate court confirmed supply with immediate effect following the first-instance deadline of two calendar days.
fields.RISK_DETERIORATION: With chemotherapy alone results would be inferior according to the treating physician; surgical option is excluded; the patient's disease is locally advanced and progressing.
fields.RISK_DEATH: The court held that the patient's rights to life and health are at stake; without Pembrolizumab the patient's overall survival prognosis is substantially worse.
fields.REQUESTED_TREATMENT: Pembrolizumab 200 mg every 21 days intravenously in combination with chemotherapy; first-line treatment for locally advanced non-small cell lung carcinoma with PD-L1 at 10%; as required by the treating physician and for the duration determined
fields.JUDICIAL_DECISION: Partially Granted — the Tribunal de Apelaciones en lo Civil de Sexto Turno: (1) confirmed the first-instance judgment ordering the Ministerio de Salud Pública to supply Pembrolizumab (Keytruda); (2) revoked the condemnation of the Fondo Nacional de Recursos, declaring its lack of passive standing and dismissing the action against it; costs by each party's order
fields.DECISION_GROUNDS: The appellate court held that the Fondo Nacional de Recursos lacks passive standing because its coverage protocol — while providing that Pembrolizumab is covered for lung cancer patients under the Fondo Nacional de Recursos — requires Programmed Death-Ligand 1 at 50% or more, and the patient's 10% Programmed Death-Ligand 1 does not meet this criterion; the Fondo Nacional de Recursos can only provide medications in accordance with the normative framework governing it, and while its restriction on Programmed Death-Ligand 1 level may lack explicit scientific justification, this does not rise to the level of manifest illegitimacy required by Law 16.011 for a successful amparo action. The court confirmed the Ministerio de Salud Pública's condemnation because the Ministerio, when petitioned with a request for a specific drug for a specific condition, has a duty to issue a scientifically grounded pronouncement — whether granting or denying — and its failure to conduct any individualised scientific analysis and instead merely deferring to registration information and the Fondo Nacional de Recursos' coverage protocol constitutes manifest illegitimacy; the patient's situation fell within the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos inclusion (advanced non-small cell bronchopulmonary carcinoma) but the Ministerio applied a Programmed Death-Ligand 1 restriction not present in the Formulario Terapéutico de Medicamentos text itself. The Suprema Corte de Justicia declared unconstitutional — by Sentencia No. 727/2022 — Article 7 paragraph 2 of Law 18.335 and the final paragraph of Article 45 of Law 18.211.
